title: The Macro Minute: Is it safe to buy the dip yet?

Happy Monday out there team 42. It's
your skipper here at Darius D to present
our macro minute for Monday October 20th
2025. Hope everyone had a great weekend.
So as always we'll start with the
executive summary from today's leadoff
morning note. If you would like to
review the analysis supporting these
conclusions and to see if you need to
make any changes in your portfolio to
remain on the right side of market risk
or to get on the right side of market
risk to the extent you have been
following KISS or Dr. Mo, you need to be
a client of 42 macro. So today's macro
question is is it safe to buy the dip
yet? Our answer with about 85% of S&P
500 firms exceeding profit forecasts
thus far in Q3 earnings season, stocks
are rebounding sharply as the weekend
break allowed for investors to take a
step back and reassess the broader
broadly bullish macro backdrop. This
comes amid President Donald Trump saying
the US will be fine with China before
both nations resume negotiations.
President Donald Trump identified rare
earths, fentanyl, and soybeans as the
US's key disputes with China ahead of
renewed talks with Treasury Secretary
Scott Besson prepared to meet Vice
Premier Leafong in Malaysia this week
and an expiring trade truce early next
month. Elsewhere, after delays from the
US government shutdown, the BLS will
publish September CBI data on Friday,
originally due October 15th, providing
the Federal Reserve officials key
inflation insight before next week's
policy meeting. The fact that inflation
is the only official benchmark data the
Fed will receive before its meeting is
concerning in the context of our staky
inflation theme and tariff pig in the
python thesis. We still view the biggest
risk of the 42 macro paradigm C bull
market over the next three to four
months as a petulant pow Fed that pushes
back too strongly on the structural
regime change that is partially required
to continue easing as inflation
accelerates um beyond 1.5 its arbitrary
2% target. This outcome would exacerbate
the developing issues in the repo market
that we discussed last week. For now,
the issues in the repo market appear
manageable, if not dissipating.
Moreover, we still maintain structural
regime change at the Fed is a high
probability outcome. So, investors
should continue buying dips until we
signal the Trump administration is back
to pursuing bearish paradigm B, which
itself is a low probability outcome.
Transitioning to our 42 micro dashboard
is always a wrap up the question from
our community. This one's not really a
question, but a fun comment I thought it
was worth sharing. This one's titled

since you started. My portfolio has been
smashing it. At any rate, my retirement
will be able to move forward. So, that's
uh that's wonderful to hear. We're
grateful to support. Uh so, wow. Today
is why I cheerfully pay that
subscription and can't wait to watch
every week. Dr. Mo, he is the man. I had
to go back to the beginning of the month
to make sure I was not imagining the
change. rolling on the floor laughing. I
see him tapping on our shoulders. Time
to put on the helmet, execute
dispatchly, and take cover. Fingers
crossed we don't get that jump condition
in the repo market. I think we could all

team for all that you do. So, thank you
guys. Appreciate every investor. We have
thousands of investors around the world
